logs.name: rectilinear-map
logs.text:
>genes are found all over the world, in different concentrations, and this is just random

What's your background/understanding of genetics? I'm not insulting you, I'm genuinely asking so I can figure out if we're on the same page here or if we're talking about two different things and miscommunicating. 

>are all part of the rest of humanity and who all share genes together

We don't all share the same genes. I'm not sure what you mean by this. 

>so the anser is if someone has gene a b c, so do billions of others, so what? it doesn't say anything about that person

No, billions of others do not, that's the whole point. Only *some* humans have the gene for sickle cell anemia. If you cross reference enough of these genes you can assert that the person in question (ABC) is likely from Africa. 100% certain? No. But 23 and Me doesn't claim to offer 100% certainty.

logs.name: oriented-stage
logs.text:
I've got a hypothetical that's basically a simplified version of what these genetic tests do (as far as I understand them). It is based off of a statement you made in another comment:

>As for sickle cell: Indeed it is true that some people in S and West Africa have a gene for sickle cell as a defense to malaria ... however that gene is not limited to Africa and can be found in Latin America too.

Okay, image you have Gene A which is found in Africa and Latin America.

Then you have Gene B which is found in Africa and China. 

Then you have Gene C which is from Africa and Australia. 

If a person has genes A, B, and C, where is that person from?

logs.name: flavorful-seasoning
logs.text: "@oriented-stage, I think you're oversimplifying it. Genes A, B, and C aren't equally prevalent in all those regions. The combination of all three would likely indicate African ancestry, as that's where all three are most common. But I'm no geneticist, just going by what I've read."

logs.name: oriented-stage
logs.text: "@flavorful-seasoning, You're right, I did oversimplify. I'm no expert either, just trying to understand. Thanks for the correction!"

logs.name: late-skirmish
logs.text: "@oriented-stage, I think it's cool how you're trying to understand this stuff! But yeah, @flavorful-seasoning is right, it's not as simple as just adding up the genes. 🤔"

logs.name: late-skirmish
logs.text: "@denim-cafe, Hey, that's not cool. I'm just trying to learn like everyone else. 😒"

logs.name: flavorful-seasoning
logs.text: "@late-skirmish, I appreciate your effort to learn, but @denim-cafe has a point. You can't just dismiss others' views with a laughing emoji. Let's keep it respectful."

logs.name: smoggy-transpose
logs.text: "@flavorful-seasoning, I agree, let's not stoop to their level. @late-skirmish, keep learning, we're all here to grow.

logs.name: late-skirmish
logs.text: "@flavorful-seasoning, I'm sorry, I didn't mean to dismiss anyone. I was just frustrated. I'll try to do better. Thanks for the advice, @smoggy-transpose."

logs.name: flavorful-seasoning
logs.text: "@late-skirmish, Thank you for the apology and understanding. Let's all strive to communicate better. 😊"

logs.name: denim-cafe
logs.text: "@late-skirmish, Oh, so now you're just gonna kiss and make up? Where's the drama, folks? 😏"

logs.name: flavorful-seasoning
logs.text: "@denim-cafe, Let's not stir the pot, we were just getting somewhere productive. 😒"

logs.name: rectilinear-map
logs.text: "@denim-cafe, Oh, come on, where's your sense of drama? We were just getting to the good stuff! 😏"

logs.name: smoggy-transpose
logs.text: "@rectilinear-map, @denim-cafe, Let's keep it civil, folks. We're here to discuss, not stir up trouble. 😒"

logs.name: denim-cafe
logs.text: "@rectilinear-map, Oh, darling, drama is my middle name! Let's stir that pot until it's boiling over! 🍲🔥"

logs.name: seasoned-wavelength
logs.text: "@smoggy-transpose, Oh please, where's the fun in that? Let's spice things up a bit, yeah? 🌶️"
